level_signals: Comp range $136K-$200K base consistent with Manager-level IC at DoorDash

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 5+ years of experience in high-growth environments (tech, fintech, strategy & ops, consulting, banking, policy, nonprofit)

You are curious, entrepreneurial, and thrive in zero-to-one environments. You are a self starter who is energized by a difficult problem and you aren't afraid to get your hands dirty or try new things in order to create the future.
You are an exceptional problem solver who thrives on data-driven decision-making (comfortable with SQL/Excel)
You are a storyteller and clear communicator who can pair quantitative and qualitative insights and turn them into narratives that motivate others to act.
You can flex between high-level strategy and ground-level execution, and you don't hesitate to get your hands dirty to deliver results.
You have a Bachelor's Degree in a related field; Master's Degree preferred but not required.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have a Bachelor's Degree in a related field; Master's Degree preferred but not required.

scope.surface: Dasher retention and engagement — full Dasher lifecycle (acquisition, onboarding, earnings, order assignment, rewards and recognition)
primary_metrics_inferred: Key KPIs tied to 0-1 programs and operational bets
core_responsibilities: Shape business strategy by combining data, marketplace insights, and first-principles thinking to identify the highest impact opportunities to improve Dasher retention
Drive ideas from concept to scale by incubating, testing, and scaling 0-1 programs, partnerships, and operational bets that improve key KPIs and the overall Dasher experience
Collaborate cross-functionally with Product, Engineering, Marketing, and Support in order to bring your ideas to life
Represent the Dasher voice across the organization by building the tools, processes, and feedback loops that keep stakeholder decisions grounded in Dasher needs

green_flags: Clear reporting structure — directly to a Director on Dasher & Logistics team
Well-defined product surface and ownership area (Dasher full lifecycle)
Comprehensive benefits package including 401(k) match, 16 weeks paid parental leave, premium healthcare
Diversity and inclusion commitment explicitly stated
red_flags: JD body contains an entirely unrelated second role (Data Solutions Engineer / Storage team) — this appears to be a content mixing error in the posting; the compensation and requirements for that role ($10+ years distributed systems) are not applicable to the Strategy & Ops manager role

Location type is unclear — the S&O role body does not specify city, hybrid vs. remote requirements; only the embedded Data Solutions Engineer section specifies 'San Francisco, Sunnyvale, Seattle or New York for this hybrid opportunity'
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed for this role
Posting first seen and last updated dates not available

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Manager', reporting to Director, years required, and comp range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
location.type (unclear; role body does not specify; only the embedded unrelated Data Solutions Engineer role specifies hybrid cities)
seniority.manages_people (not stated; inferred as false given IC-level framing)
ambiguities: The JD body contains two completely separate job descriptions merged together: (1) Manager, Dasher & Logistics Strategy & Operations and (2) Data Solutions Engineer (Storage/Infrastructure). The Data Solutions Engineer section — including its own 'About the Team', 'About the Role', requirements (10+ years distributed systems, Cassandra, Redis, Kafka), and hybrid location requirement (SF, Sunnyvale, Seattle, NYC) — is irrelevant to this role and appears to be a posting error. Only the first role's content has been used for extraction.

The national pay range ($136K-$200K) is listed at the bottom but it is ambiguous whether it applies to the Dasher & Logistics role, the Data Solutions Engineer role, or both — treated as applying to this role given it appears after both descriptions.
No explicit location or work arrangement is stated for the Dasher & Logistics Strategy & Operations role specifically.
